author: @petermines3575
text: I build them with the motor behind the rear wheel it gives me more leg room and the batteries are at the front so the floor board is lower to the ground very nice frame

author: @aaronfranklin324
text:
I've used that motor for a variety of electric trikes etc. I've had 3 variants,  24v 500w, 36v 800w, and 36v 500w with stock Hduty 6 to 1 planet reduction head. Believe they are actually the same motor all 3.
Appropriate gearing on 36V and geared for a top speed of 40kmph it smokes up a minimoto wheel on the tarseal, shreds lawns in 3 wheel drifting sod hurling shredrampages, and kicks car butt at traffic lights and city driving.
It can pull a stall current of 200A!
 I have seen 2.5kW on 24V off the line.
I think you have a gearing issue. Mine works good with 9x 80 tooth minibike sprockets and similar wheel radius, three car batterys. That's 40kmph.
Towing up to a ton it works great geared for a walking pace top speed.

Those little batteries won't give you any range or the amps your motor wants. You can kill your speed controller because of this too.  A cheap 40A controller handled over a year of such abuse. But it and another like it went toes up trying to supply current pulses the motor wanted at low throttle.
An "overdrive" spade switch or relay to bypass the controller is a good idea. It will double your range, and is much faster and torqueyer.

author: @esaedvik
text: I have a similar scooter that looks DIY, but was made by an actual company. It used 2x 12V motorcycle batteries under the plate you stand on for low center of gravity (you seem to have a nice spot for smaller batteries there as well. BMS was in a case a bit higher up. I've been meaning to update it to use 18650 cells and maybe swap out the motor as well. Cool project!

text:
Very nice Luke. You will need to double or triple the battery volume for a decent journry, that depends of course how long you intend traveling (unless of course this is only a prototype). Also a weatherproof battery housing would help.
Overall an excellent build mate,pay yourself on the back bloke.

author: @infinitelyuniversal2390
text:
Chain tensioner should be on the bottom half of chain drive,  due to help guide the chain to stay inline with sprocket, because when chain tightens under load,   your bottom half of chain gets loose and sloppy,  which won't be a problem till the road gets bumpy,   

Well done 👏thow, my next dream build is electric scooter aswell.

author: @thefirstmissinglink
text: Couldn't tell for sure but it appears you have your derailleur tensioner on the torque side of the chain. That being the case, you might end up with a lot of slack upon acceleration with the potential of throwing the chain. I like the idea of being able to shift gears. If your gear cluster freewheels then I can see the potential to save that option. If it does not then you may have to go with a single gear the best supports your load and go with a fixed tensioner. Glad to see it moving under its own power!

replies.author: @ThisRandomUsername
replies.text: Yeah, that's going to need fixing. Luke, if you're reading, you really don't need a derailleur if you're not changing gear. Get the alignment right, slot the motor mounting holes a bit and tension it correctly and you'll be good.

author: @jj74qformerlyjailbreak3
text:
Hey Bub 
I’m Taking The Gear Box Off My 47cc Scooter. I’m Setting A Clutch On The Shaft Of My Motor. 
Changing The Springs To Less Tension Has Giving Me The Grab I Want. I’m Using Robs Computational Circuit For Speed. 
Cheers Mate. 
Learning Is The EndGame.

author: @zaybxc0
text: this channel has been making supercaps and batteries  it baffles me why your projects would use sponsored batteries on a DIY project. For me it is very disappointing.

replies.author: @naotamf1588
replies.text: convienience i suppose, he would have used the two lead acid batteries otherwise. Rob made 2 different large carbon ink batteries for previous videos and was able to drive a ~60V comercially available scooter with one. Take theier videos as concept steps, you chose to learn from each one according to your interests and own projects.

replies.author: @zaybxc0
replies.text: @naotamf1588 To be honest I am just concerned . Rob is the only Youtuber that has the content that he has and there is so much more I thought he would have explored especially in light of the emerging technologies in solid and flat batteries. He certainly can make his own choice yet we have supported him mostly because of batteries. I just wonder if he is being suppressed or he really only wants to do other things alone. Many of us were waiting to support his shop with the batteries he was selling. I personally would have invested much in them even if there were not tested. This is Historic if you really put it in perspective . This was the opportunity for the layman to change the face of the world . Batteries is the cornerstone of our civilization. I just surprised no one else sees that.

author: @nil5221
text: Good job Luke. One improvement: the idler tensioner should be on the slack part of the chain drive when the motor is powering. As you've installed it, when the motor powers, the top portion will pull tight, and the bottom portion will go slack because that's where the tensioner should be.  I.e. under power, the chain will be loose. Hope that makes sense.
